steps.title: Separate the independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence first says jetties can have the opposite effect. It then gives a complete explanation: obstructing sand flow can increase erosion. Those two independent clauses need a semicolon, while though is set off after effect.

steps.title: Reject faulty boundaries
steps.content:
• Choice B lacks the semicolon needed between the two independent clauses.
  • Choice C places though after the semicolon, leaving the second side as an awkward dependent construction.
  • Choice D uses commas only, creating a comma splice between independent clauses.
The answer is A.
